auth.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Reload the page to sign in again.
auth.forbiddenScopeMessage: This action requires the `{{scope}}` permission. Contact your administrator or check the operator runbook.
auth.forbiddenGenericMessage: You don't have permission to perform this action. Contact your administrator.

today.title: Today
today.empty_no_match: Nothing matches the current filters. Widen the date range or clear the search.
today.usage_card_label: Token usage in the last 24 hours

intro.purpose: Live operations overview — a real-time snapshot of what your agent is doing right now: in-flight sessions, recent activity, token spend and the health of background workers.
intro.usage: Leave it open as a dashboard. It auto-refreshes on a timer; tick “Pause auto-refresh” to freeze the view, or use Refresh to pull the latest immediately.

usage.title: Token Usage
usage.empty_no_rows: No usage in this range. Widen the date range.

intro.purpose: Token spend report — how many tokens (and roughly how much) each model, session and channel has consumed, so you can see where your LLM budget is going.
intro.usage: Pick a time range and optional filters, then read the totals and per-model breakdown. Pair it with HotL Policies to cap the lines that cost the most.

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.description: Cron / interval / webhook / file-watch / proactive jobs. The runner picks up every change on the next tick (default 30s).
scheduler.tokens_description: Tokens for the public webhook endpoint at POST /v1/scheduler/webhooks/:route_id. Pass via X-Xiaoguai-Token header.
scheduler.tokens_new_token: New token (capture now — won’t be shown again):
scheduler.create_placeholder_description: e.g. scan r/LocalLLaMA + HN at 8am daily, push result to Telegram

eval.title: Eval
eval.description: Regression + capability suites against the same MockBackend the CLI uses. Reports stay on disk; the console caches the last verdict per suite.
eval.suites_empty_none: No suites under the configured directory.
eval.run_pick_prompt: Pick a suite from the left to run it.
eval.convert_description: Project a production sessions.id into a ready-to-edit .eval.yaml case. The server suggests assertions; review + paste into a file under suites_dir/.

intro.purpose: Your activity history on this agent — every action, what and when. Filter by type or search to find anything; each entry is tamper-checked so you can trust the record.
intro.usage: Filter or search to find an action, then click a row for details. Need a formal compliance report? Use Export.

mcp_servers.title: MCP Servers
mcp_servers.empty_none: No MCP servers registered. Use xiaoguai mcp register --name ... --transport stdio --command ... to add one.

intro.purpose: MCP servers — the external tool servers (Model Context Protocol) currently wired into your agent, giving it capabilities like file access, search or office-doc conversion.
intro.usage: This is a read-only inventory of registered servers and their transport. To add servers, install from the MCP Marketplace pane.

marketplace.title: MCP Marketplace
marketplace.description: Curated MCP servers. Click Install to add one to your mcp_servers table; configure required env vars out-of-band. Catalog version {{version}}.

providers.title: LLM Providers
providers.hint: Point at a local model URL (Ollama / OpenAI-compatible server) or a hosted API (MiniMax, Zhipu, OpenAI, DeepSeek…). API keys are stored server-side and never shown again.
providers.restart_hint: New providers and edits take effect after the server restarts (the router is built at boot).
providers.api_key_placeholder: API key (leave blank for local URLs)
providers.api_key_keep_placeholder: Leave blank to keep the existing key
providers.empty: No providers yet. Add one above.
providers.probe_all_failed: No models connected — check the API key, endpoint, and model names.

intro.purpose: LLM providers — register the models your agent can call: a local server (Ollama / OpenAI-compatible) or a hosted API (MiniMax, Zhipu, OpenAI, DeepSeek…). API keys are stored server-side and never shown again.
intro.usage: Add a provider with its base URL, key and model, or edit an existing one. Changes take effect after the server restarts, since the model router is built at boot.

outcomes.title: Outcomes
outcomes.empty_no_records: No outcomes recorded in this range.
outcomes.session_empty_hint: Click a row in the List tab to drill into a session chain, or enter a session ID above.
outcomes.error_503: Outcomes backend unavailable (PgOutcomeRecorder not wired — run with a Postgres DB to enable).

intro.purpose: Business outcomes ledger — records the concrete results your agent produced (deals closed, tickets resolved, dollars earned/saved), linked back to the session that produced them.
intro.usage: Filter by kind, session or agent to find an outcome, then click a row to drill into the originating session and its provenance chain.

hotl_policies.title: HotL Policies
hotl_policies.tier_autonomous_desc: No budget limit — the agent acts without a cap. Highest trust, highest blast radius.
hotl_policies.tier_gated_desc: Autonomous up to the budget, then escalates to a human for approval.
hotl_policies.tier_strict_desc: Acts up to the budget, then hard-denies on breach — no human in the loop.
hotl_policies.unavailable_body: PgHotlPolicyStore is not yet wired — the API returned 503. This is expected in dev mode without Postgres. Check bridges status:

intro.purpose: Human-on-the-Loop (HotL) policies — budget and rate limits on governed actions. When an action exceeds a policy's bounds it is escalated to a human (or denied) instead of running unchecked, keeping the agent safe and affordable.
intro.usage: Create a policy with a `scope` (which action it guards), a `window_seconds` time window, and the ceilings `max_count` (how many times) and/or `max_usd` (how much spend) allowed in that window; set `escalate_to` to the channel that gets notified on breach. Use Test policy to dry-run a hypothetical action against your rules.
intro.example_1: Cap `llm_call`: at most 10 calls per 60 seconds (`window_seconds: 60`, `max_count: 10`); on breach `escalate_to: ops` so the ops channel is pinged.
intro.example_2: Cap `tool_call.git_push`: at most 5 per day (`window_seconds: 86400`, `max_count: 5`) so the agent can't push in a loop.

loops.title: Self-driving loops

intro.purpose: Self-driving loops are the agent's autonomous heartbeat: a loop re-runs a prompt on a session at a cadence until a budget (ticks, time-to-live or tokens) is reached, or you stop it. This is where the background "/loop" runs become visible.
intro.usage: Watch a loop's status and next-tick time to confirm it is making progress. Resume a paused loop, or Cancel one that has gone off track. Loops start from chat ("/loop") — they are listed here for monitoring and control.

loops.unwired: Loops are not wired on this server, so there is nothing to monitor yet.
loops.empty: No loops yet. Start one from chat with "/loop" and it will appear here.

anomaly.title: Anomaly back-test

intro.purpose: Anomaly detection surfaces unusual spikes in a numeric time-series — token spend, error rate, order volume — that may signal a runaway loop, a misconfiguration or abuse. This pane back-tests a detector against sample data so you can see, on the spot, which points it would flag and how confident it is.
intro.usage: Pick a detector and threshold, paste a CSV (or load a sample), then Run back-test. Each flagged point is shown with its z-score. Live, scheduled detection is configured per pack and runs on the Scheduler — use Run now there to fire a check immediately.

anomaly.result_none: No anomalies fired for this data and detector.
anomaly.live_body: This back-test is offline and on-demand. Live, continuous detection runs as a scheduled job installed by a skill pack — open the Scheduler to see those jobs, and use "Run now" there to trigger a check without waiting for the next tick.

memory.title: Memory
memory.list_empty: No memories match the current filters.
memory.delete_confirm_body: This memory may be important to agents. Are you sure you want to delete:
memory.recall_description: Test semantic recall: enter a query and see which memories the agent would recall, ranked by similarity.
memory.recall_empty: No memories recalled for this query.
memory.neighbors_description: Find memories nearest by vector similarity. High-similarity neighbors may be duplicates or conflicts worth reconciling.

intro.purpose: Long-term memory store — the facts and notes your agent has saved and can recall in future sessions. This is the agent's persistent knowledge, editable by hand.
intro.usage: Browse or search recalled memories, edit or delete entries inline, and use the import/export toolbar to move memory in or out as JSONL.

personas.title: Personas
personas.subtitle: Named role profiles attached to sessions (sprint-9 triangle: planner / worker / critic).
personas.delete_confirm_body: Archiving prevents this persona from being attached to new sessions. Existing sessions keep their persona attachment.
personas.unavailable: Personas repository not configured (HTTP 503). Wire `xiaoguai-personas::pg::PgPersonaRepository` in the API host.

intro.purpose: Personas — named role profiles (a system prompt plus a style) that you can attach to a chat to give the agent a specific voice and behavior, e.g. a planner, a worker or a critic.
intro.usage: Create a persona with its role, prompt and style here, then bind it to a conversation from the chat UI's persona selector. Personas are also the building blocks of Expert Teams.

expert_teams.title: Expert Teams
expert_teams.subtitle: Named persona compositions with a designated lead. Until parallel orchestration lands, an attached team runs the session with its lead persona.
expert_teams.members_none_available: No active personas available — create personas first.
expert_teams.hint_no_members: Select at least one member persona.
expert_teams.hint_lead_not_member: Choose a lead from the selected members.
expert_teams.delete_confirm_body: Archiving prevents this team from being attached to new sessions. Existing sessions keep their team attachment.
expert_teams.empty_filtered: No teams match the filter. Clear the search to see all teams.
expert_teams.cta_body: A team is a named group of personas with one lead. Once created, you can run it from the chat UI to tackle a task together.
expert_teams.cta_step_personas_ready: Pick the roles (personas) you want on the team — you already have some defined.
expert_teams.cta_step_personas_missing: First create the roles (personas) you need — a team needs at least one member.
expert_teams.cta_step_create: Create a team here: choose its members and a lead.
expert_teams.cta_step_run: Open the chat UI, pick the team in the Expert selector, and run a task together.
expert_teams.cta_personas_hint: No active personas yet — head to the Personas page to create one first, then come back.
expert_teams.unavailable: Teams repository not configured (HTTP 503). Wire the teams repository in the API host.
expert_teams.field_glossary: Team glossary (markdown — injected into every team session turn)
expert_teams.glossary_over_cap: Glossary is {{bytes}} bytes — over the 16 KiB limit ({{max}} bytes). The server will reject the save.

intro.purpose: Expert Teams — a named group of personas with one designated lead. Bound to a conversation, the members collaborate (in parallel as orchestration lands) and the lead synthesizes their work into one answer.
intro.usage: First create the roles you need in Personas, then here create a team, pick its members and choose a lead. Finally bind the team from the chat UI's Expert selector.
intro.example_1: A “code-review squad”: members `security-reviewer` + `performance-reviewer` + `style-reviewer`, lead = `tech-lead`, which gathers each reviewer's notes and produces one consolidated review.

incidents.title: Incidents
incidents.subtitle: Self-healing incidents (#277): triage, review the RCA, and approve or dismiss the repair. Manual incidents are owner-authed — no webhook token.
incidents.placeholder_title: e.g. Disk full on backup host
incidents.approve_confirm_body: The Executor will run the approved RCA's action items and may change system state. This is the human approval gate.
incidents.dismiss_confirm_body: Dismissing closes the incident without acting. This is terminal — it can't be reopened.
incidents.unavailable: Incident store not configured (HTTP 503). Wire the incident store in the API host.

intro.purpose: Incidents — the self-healing review board. Alerts (Sentry/Datadog webhooks) and manual reports land here; you review the agent's root-cause analysis and decide whether to approve the repair.
intro.usage: Filter by status, open an incident, and Analyze to get an RCA. Review it, then Approve repair to let the Executor act — or Dismiss to close it. Every action is audited.
intro.example_1: A Datadog “disk full” alert opens an incident → Analyze drafts an RCA → you Approve repair → the Executor runs the fix and the incident resolves.

skill_proposals.title: Skill Proposals
skill_proposals.subtitle: Review agent-authored skill manifests (DEC-014). Approving writes the manifest to disk and flips the row to `installed`; rejecting records a reason that goes back to the agent.
skill_proposals.unavailable: Skill proposals repository not configured (HTTP 503). Wire `xiaoguai_api::AppState::skill_proposals` in the API host.
skill_proposals.empty: No proposals match the current filter.
skill_proposals.approve_body: Approving installs the manifest `{{name}}@{{version}}` to the skills directory. The agent will be allowed to use it from the next session start.
skill_proposals.reject_body: Rejecting marks `{{name}}` as denied and surfaces the reason back to the proposing agent.
skill_proposals.reject_reason_placeholder: e.g. tool allowlist overlaps with a restricted scope
skill_proposals.reject_reason_required: A reason is required so the agent can learn from the rejection.

intro.purpose: Skill proposals — a review queue for skills the agent has drafted for itself (DEC-014). Nothing is installed until you approve it, keeping the agent governable.
intro.usage: Open a proposal to inspect its manifest. Approving writes the skill to disk and marks it installed; rejecting records a reason that is sent back to the agent.

skill_packs.title: Skill Packs
skill_packs.manage_note_body: Day-to-day skill discovery and activation live in the chat "Skills" page — the ability center for using skills. Here you manage what's installed: the pack inventory, activation status, and Rescan & activate.
skill_packs.template_disclaimer: These are skill templates — installing records your configuration; running a skill needs its data source or an MCP server.
skill_packs.activation_notice_body: Packs with conversational agents activate their agent team on the next serve boot; to skip the restart, click "Rescan & activate" below to activate them now. Packs that only ship anomaly/watch monitors (e.g. observability-starter) form no conversational team and stay "activation pending".
skill_packs.rescan_hint: After installing a pack with conversational agents, click to activate its team immediately (no serve restart).
skill_packs.rescan_noop: No new conversational teams to activate.
skill_packs.catalog_hint: Pick a pre-built pack from the catalog and install it in one click.
skill_packs.manual_heading: Or record a pack by ID
skill_packs.confirm_note: A pack with conversational agents activates its team on the next serve boot, or when you click "Rescan & activate".
skill_packs.installed_empty: No skill packs recorded yet. Use the controls above to install a pack.
skill_packs.detail_activation_note: no conversational agent team is active yet (no conversational agents, or not scanned).
skill_packs.detail_activation_note_active: the conversational agent team is active and usable in orchestration / chat.

intro.purpose: Skill packs — bundles of reusable agent skills you can install from the catalog to extend what your agent knows how to do.
intro.usage: Browse the catalog and click Install to add a pack. Newly installed packs become active after the next agent run.

branding.title: Branding
branding.intro: Set the assistant's display name shown across the chat UI (logo, welcome line, message box). Leave empty to use the built-in default.
branding.hint: Takes effect on the next chat reload. Up to 64 characters.
